project_structure: Three-tier architecture: Frontend (novel_agent/web/static/*.js) → Backend API (novel_agent/web/app.py) → Configuration Layer (novel_agent/config.py, novel_agent/agent_config.py) → Storage (.env file, JSON configs). Settings data flows from UI forms through POST/GET endpoints to file-based persistence.

relevant_files.rationale: Core dataflow handler: Contains GET /api/settings (lines 277-287) and POST /api/settings (lines 289-343) endpoints that manage API config persistence to .env file. This is the primary integration point for settings save/load operations.

Configuration loading root: load_dotenv() call at line 20 only runs ONCE at module import. LLMConfig class (lines 23-29) reads from os.getenv() which is populated at import time. This is the ROOT CAUSE - .env changes don't reload until server restart.

Multi-API configuration manager with JSON persistence (global_api_config.json). Lines 175-191 show _load_configs() and _load_global_config() called during __init__. Methods _save_global_config() (lines 284-294) persist to JSON but runtime config object isn't reloaded after save.

Frontend settings UI: loadGlobalAPISettings() function loads current settings on tab switch. Contains save button handlers that POST to /api/settings but likely don't refresh the config object after save.
Main application entry point: DOMContentLoaded listener at line 5001 initializes app. May need to trigger config reload after settings save to reflect changes in runtime.

patterns: Configuration Load Pattern: (1) Module import time load_dotenv() → (2) os.getenv() cached in LLMConfig defaults → (3) Runtime mutations don't persist → (4) Page refresh re-loads unchanged defaults. Save Pattern: POST endpoint writes .env file but config object remains stale. Fix Pattern: Need hot-reload mechanism - either reload_dotenv() after save or add config.reload() method called by POST endpoint.
dependencies: python-dotenv (load_dotenv), pydantic (BaseModel for config), FastAPI (request/response), Path (file I/O). Key dependency: load_dotenv() only loads once at module import, not on-demand.
integration_points: POST /api/settings (app.py:290) - saves to .env but doesn't trigger config reload → GET /api/settings (app.py:277) - returns stale config object → LLMConfig.__init__ (config.py:23-29) - only reads os.getenv once at module load → Frontend loadGlobalAPISettings() - displays config without knowing it's stale. Critical gap: No bridge between .env write and config object reload.
constraints: 1) load_dotenv() is one-time at import, cannot reload without restarting process or using dotenv_values() override=True. 2) Pydantic BaseModel defaults are set at class definition, not per-instance. 3) .env file format must be preserved during updates (app.py:309-325). 4) Multiple config systems exist: .env (config.py) and JSON (agent_config.py) - need consistent reload strategy. 5) FastAPI single-instance config object shared across all requests.

clarification_needs.question: Should settings persistence include hot-reload (config takes effect immediately without server restart) or is save-for-next-start acceptable?
clarification_needs.context: Current implementation writes .env but config object stays stale. Hot-reload requires either (a) calling dotenv_values(override=True) to refresh os.environ, or (b) adding config.reload() method and calling from POST endpoint. Option (a) is immediate but affects entire process; option (b) is cleaner but requires updating all config access points.
options: Hot-reload: Settings take effect immediately, reload config object after POST /api/save
Save-for-restart: Settings persist to .env but require server restart to take effect (minimal code change)
Hybrid: API config hot-reloads immediately, other settings require restart (selective reload)

clarification_needs.question: Which configuration system should be the single source of truth - .env file (config.py) or JSON files (agent_config.py)?
clarification_needs.context: Two parallel config systems exist: config.py uses .env (OPENAI_API_KEY, etc.) while agent_config.py uses JSON (global_api_config.json). Current POST /api/settings writes to .env, but agent_config.py also has its own save/load. Need to decide if they should be unified or kept separate.
options: Unify to .env only: Migrate all config to .env, deprecate JSON configs (simpler, industry standard)
Keep dual system: .env for infrastructure, JSON for application settings (current pattern, more complex)
Migrate to JSON only: Replace .env with JSON configs (easier hot-reload, loses env var benefits)

clarification_needs.question: For TDD approach, what is the expected behavior after saving settings - should the GET /api/settings endpoint return the newly saved values immediately?
clarification_needs.context: Test-driven development requires defining expected post-save behavior. If hot-reload is implemented, GET /api/settings called immediately after POST should return new values. This determines whether tests need to verify immediate persistence or delayed (next-restart) persistence.
options: Immediate: GET /api/settings returns new values right after POST (hot-reload required)
Delayed: GET returns old values until restart, but .env file contains new values (current behavior)
Hybrid: Add new endpoint GET /api/settings?source=file to read from disk vs memory

_metadata.task_description: TDD: Settings Persistence Fix - Fix AGENT API configuration and other settings not persisting after page refresh
